summary: An IQ score of 90-110 classifies as average cognition; recent research highlights the importance of comprehensive assessments including functional capabilities beyond just test results. The primary focus has shifted toward understanding how well these individuals function in daily life rather than solely based on their standardized test scores. This aligns closely with maintaining "average" designation while acknowledging broader context influences interpretation significantly less rigidly compared before. To reiterate concisely but accurately reflects current consensus among experts regarding such classifications. \(\blacksquare\) Agent2 concluded after reviewing additional information emphasizing modern approaches integrating multiple facets when evaluating human intellect. Despite slight adjustments due newer insights focusing more broadly at overall functionality instead narrow metrics alone, fundamental placement under “normal”/“average” spectrum holds firm given
